# Implementation Plan: Post-Write Digest Validator

**Branch**: `004-post-write-validator` | **Date**: 2026-03-21 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-post-write-validator/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by `/speckit.plan`.

## Summary

Add `validate_digest.py` to `.claude/skills/daily-digest/scripts/` — a Python stdlib script that validates any written digest file against the format contract (section counts, evidence fields, formatting, low-signal warning consistency). The script is called by the `daily-digest` orchestrator immediately after `write_digest.py` and can also be invoked standalone on any existing digest. Validation failure exits with code 1 and blocks the orchestrator run; the report is printed to stdout and written alongside the digest as `<filename>.validation.txt`.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.8+, stdlib only
**Primary Dependencies**: `re`, `sys`, `os`, `pathlib`, `datetime` (all stdlib)
**Storage**: File system — reads digest `.md` files (read-only); writes `.validation.txt` report alongside
**Testing**: Manual invocation against fixture digest files; exit code assertions
**Target Platform**: Claude Code CLI environment (cross-platform: Windows, macOS, Linux)
**Project Type**: CLI utility script
**Performance Goals**: Completes within 2 seconds for any single digest file
**Constraints**: Python stdlib only; no third-party packages; read-only access to digest; cross-platform paths
**Scale/Scope**: Single file per invocation; no concurrency

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Gate | Principle | Status |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Delivery vehicle: feature delivered as `.claude/skills/<name>/<name>.md` | I | ❌ |
| Skill format: frontmatter + User Input + Outline present | I + II | ✅ |
| Script scope: scripts perform I/O only, no business logic | II | ❌ |
| Reference material in `resources/`, not inline | II | ✅ |
| Evidence requirement: all insights include direct quote | III | ✅ |
| Count enforcement within 1–3/2–4/1–3/3–5 ranges | III | ✅ |
| Partial failure returns digest with status, not error | IV | ✅ |
| Preflight checks verify hard deps before discovery | IV | ✅ |
| Python stdlib only; no third-party packages | V | ✅ |

*Post-design re-check: both violations remain; both are justified (see Complexity Tracking).*

```text
.claude/skills/daily-digest/
├── daily-digest.md                    # Modified: add validate_digest.py call in Step 9
└── scripts/
    ├── check_runtime.py               # Existing — unchanged
    ├── validate_input.py              # Existing — unchanged
    ├── build_path.py                  # Existing — unchanged
    ├── write_digest.py                # Existing — unchanged
    └── validate_digest.py             # NEW: post-write validator

tests/
└── fixtures/                          # NEW: digest fixture files for manual testing
    ├── digest-valid.md
    ├── digest-missing-evidence.md
    ├── digest-count-violations.md
    ├── digest-broken-formatting.md
    └── digest-low-signal-missing-warning.md
```

**Structure Decision**: Single project (Option 1). All new code lives in the existing `scripts/` directory alongside the other utility scripts. No new directories in the skill tree; `tests/fixtures/` is added as a testing resource.

## Complexity Tracking

| Violation | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |
|-----------|------------|-------------------------------------|
| Delivery vehicle: script, not skill | Validation must be deterministic across runs; Claude reasoning is non-deterministic. The user-facing entry point remains the `/validate-digest` skill; this script is a sub-component it calls. | A pure skill implementation would produce different pass/fail results on identical digests depending on model temperature and phrasing, making it untestable and unreliable as a hard gate. |
| Script scope: contains validation logic | Format validation requires deterministic rule-checking (regex, counting, structural parsing) that cannot be delegated to Claude reasoning. `validate_input.py` sets the same precedent: it contains topic-format validation logic beyond pure I/O. | Moving all logic into the skill Outline would make the orchestrator responsible for parsing digest files mid-execution, mixing generation and validation concerns and making the skill significantly harder to read and test. |
